_meta.description: Municipality Participation Database — Layer 3 backbone for the Permit Intelligence Platform
source_notes: Data sourced from Parolo index, lokaleregelgeving.overheid.nl, and individual municipality portals
All policies apply to BOPA (Buitenplanse Omgevingsactiviteit) applications under the Omgevingswet
Legal basis: Art. 16.55 Omgevingswet + Art. 7.4 Omgevingsregeling

v2.0.0 (2026-04-20): +10 municipalities (Delft, Amersfoort, Venlo, Zoetermeer, Helmond, Leeuwarden, Zaanstad, Deventer, Ede, Dordrecht). Corrections: Tilburg (Impactscan), Apeldoorn (binary_list), Nijmegen (binary_list resolved), Enschede (7-category list mapped), Utrecht (process_mandatory).

v2.1.0 (2026-04-29): +13 municipalities. Reconciliation: Sittard-Geleen, Westland, Emmen (added from frontend). New: Haarlemmermeer, Alkmaar, Alphen aan den Rijn, Hengelo, Gouda, Roosendaal, Lelystad, Purmerend, Hoorn, Nissewaard.

scoring_system.description: Developer answers a 4-question questionnaire → point score → category assigned (Participatiehandreiking 2022)
scoring_system.source: Participatiehandreiking 'Betrek de buurt bij uw initiatieven' — Gemeente Amsterdam (2022)

questions.question_nl: Wat is het type/omvang van uw initiatief?
questions.question_en: What is the type/scale of your initiative?

questions.question_nl: Zorgt uw initiatief voor meer verkeer of parkeerdrukte?
questions.question_en: Does your initiative cause more traffic or parking pressure?

questions.question_nl: Heeft uw initiatief gevolgen voor het groen?
questions.question_en: Does your initiative affect green space?

questions.question_nl: Verandert uw initiatief de beleving van de wijk of buurt?
questions.question_en: Does your initiative change the experience of the neighbourhood?

options.label_nl: Verandering van de beleving van de wijk of buurt
Grote veranderingen van de beleving van de wijk of buurt

categories.label: Beperkte gevolgen
categories.description: Inform direct neighbours and immediate residents only

categories.label: Middelgrote gevolgen
categories.description: Consult broader group including neighbours, residents, and local entrepreneurs

categories.label: Aanzienlijke gevolgen
categories.description: Active advisory process — must involve gebiedsmakelaar, must motivate any deviations from advice

municipalities.document_notes: Amsterdam uses 'participatieplan' (not 'participatieverslag'). Must match category depth. Results shared with municipality AND stakeholders.
municipalities.strictness_notes: Strictest municipality in the Netherlands. No exceptions. Score system determines intensity.
municipalities.product_play: The scoring questionnaire is fully replicable as a product feature. Amsterdam is the best MVP demo case.

scale_system.description: Large initiatives = mandatory with intake team. Small = encouraged, lighter touch.

tracks.description: Full mandatory participation with professional support
Lighter touch with factsheet, encouraged but not strictly mandatory

scale_system.size_threshold_notes: Threshold between large/small not explicitly published — intake team assessment determines track

municipalities.document_notes: Required for large initiatives only
municipalities.prior_framework_exemption_notes: If project aligns with an existing approved spatial framework (masterplan, urban vision), no fresh participation required — participation already occurred at the planning stage. Major time-saver.
municipalities.strictness_notes: High for large projects, lighter for small. Prior-framework exemption reduces burden significantly.
municipalities.product_play: Auto-flag whether a project qualifies for the prior-framework exemption — major value-add.
research_gaps: Exact definition of 'large' vs 'small' initiative threshold

designated_system.description: Council must formally designate which project types require mandatory participation. List still being finalised as of 2026.
designated_system.designation_list_notes: A court ruled Sep 2024 that no mandatory participation applied to a specific project because council had not formally designated it. Policy was ambiguous through all of 2024. Published decisions and ordinances are findable via the Den Haag Gemeenteblad.

municipalities.document_notes: Required for designated categories only
municipalities.strictness_notes: Policy adopted Dec 2024, in effect March 2025. Designation list still maturing — treat as uncertain until full list is published.
municipalities.product_play: High value in providing up-to-date Den Haag designations as they are finalised. Monitor this municipality closely.
research_gaps: Full designation list of mandatory project types under the 2024 ordinance

process_system.description: No fixed project-type trigger yet. Developers must run the 'kompas voor zeggenschap' — a decision-making compass guiding which cooperation form is appropriate. Functionally mandatory for all significant BOPAs.
process_system.incoming_change: Utrecht is transitioning to a hybrid model. The Beleidsregel Vergunningenstrategie Omgevingswet (effective 18-03-2026, para. 3.3) explicitly states a BOPA designation list will be submitted to council in Q1 2026. Once published, architecture will shift from process_mandatory to a hybrid process_mandatory + binary_list.
process_system.incoming_change_status: Q1/Q2 2026 council decision — not yet published as of April 20, 2026
process_system.wrong_source_note: CVDR721904 ('Verordening re-integratie en tegenprestatie Participatiewet') is an employment ordinance under social welfare law — irrelevant to spatial planning. Do not use.

municipalities.document_notes: Samenwerkingsplan (before) + samenwerkingsverslag (after). Both required.
municipalities.policy_document_type: Beleidsnota 'Samen stad maken' + Verordening samenwerking participatie inspraak (10-04-2025)
municipalities.strictness_notes: Civic culture is strong — even voluntary participation is de facto expected. Kompas requires genuine deliberation, not checkbox exercise.
municipalities.product_play: Utrecht needs a guided workflow tool more than a binary checker now. Walk developers through the kompas questions → output samenwerkingsplan template. Add binary checker once the designation list is published.
research_gaps: Utrecht BOPA designation list — Q1/Q2 2026 council decision not yet published. Monitor lokaleregelgeving.overheid.nl.

quickscan_system.description: Developer uses municipality's Quickscan tool to determine if participation is required and at what level. Large projects go through intake team with participation advisor.

municipalities.document_notes: Who was involved, how participation occurred, what was done with input
municipalities.strictness_notes: Only municipality with a self-serve Quickscan tool — the benchmark for developer tooling.
municipalities.product_play: The product is essentially 'Eindhoven's Quickscan for all 342 municipalities.' This is the clearest product positioning.
research_gaps: Exact Quickscan parameters and thresholds — need to run through the tool

impactscan_system.description: All BOPAs mandatory. Intensity determined by Impactscan across 6 themes → Level A/B/C/D.
impactscan_system.source: CVDR738554 — Aanwijzen gevallen van verplichte participatie bij een omgevingsvergunning voor een buitenplanse omgevingsplanactiviteit

levels.label: Aanzienlijke gevolgen
levels.description: Advisory process with independent facilitation (not municipality-led)

levels.label: Aanzienlijke gevolgen — strategisch gebied
levels.description: Advisory process where municipality acts as active co-partner (not just facilitator)

impactscan_system.scoring_formula_note: Exact per-theme scoring weights not yet extracted from CVDR738554 — impactscan scoring is heuristic until formula is documented

municipalities.document_notes: Report must document: intensity level chosen, participation process, outcomes, and how feedback was incorporated into the plan.
municipalities.strictness_notes: Blanket mandatory — no exceptions. The 4-level Impactscan makes this the second fully modellable scoring system found (alongside Amsterdam). Level D for strategic zones is particularly demanding.
municipalities.product_play: Tilburg's Impactscan is the second replicable scoring system after Amsterdam. Also high value for the participatieverslag generator — Impactscan level determines report depth.
research_gaps: Exact per-theme scoring scale and weights from CVDR738554 — needed for precise Level A/B/C/D assignment

case_by_case_system.description: No fixed threshold or list. Municipality assesses each application individually. If participation is found insufficient, municipality collects stakeholder views itself — causing delay at minimum.

municipalities.document_notes: Who was involved, how, how their interests were addressed
municipalities.strictness_notes: Most ambiguous of all profiled municipalities. Policy still being defined.
municipalities.product_play: Highest immediate pain for developers in Almere. Ambiguity = maximum confusion = maximum product value.
municipalities.special_note: This is dad's primary municipality — highest personal relevance.
research_gaps: Final policy document once published
Exact triggers and standards once policy is finalised

binary_list_system.description: 14-category binary activities list. On the list = mandatory participation. Not on the list = optional.

categories.activity: Renovation / modification / expansion of existing housing and buildings

categories.threshold: Village population <5,000 residents AND >2 homes organic growth

categories.activity: Converting recreational homes to permanent residence
Second service dwelling for agricultural business

categories.threshold: Any increase of existing land area
Building in gaps in existing settlement patterns
Welfare, healthcare, culture, education, sports, childcare — all cases
ALL THREE conditions simultaneously: terrain >100m² AND structure >5m AND mast >40m
Kiosks, pavilions, temporary cultural or commercial buildings
Waterways for vessel moorage including houseboats
Solar fields, wind turbines, biogas installations

municipalities.document_notes: Covers who was involved, what was communicated, what feedback was received, and how it was handled
municipalities.strictness_notes: Most structured municipal participation system in the Netherlands. Binary list leaves no ambiguity.
municipalities.product_play: Groningen's structure is the ideal product database schema. Every municipality should eventually have this level of structured data.

two_document_system.description: Unique requirement: TWO documents at different stages of the process

documents.name: Participation Report
contents: what was not incorporated and why

municipalities.document_notes: Both documents required — plan before, report after. Unique in the Netherlands.
municipalities.non_compliance_notes: If insufficient, justification for zoning deviation fails → permit denied
municipalities.strictness_notes: Two-document requirement is the most structured submission requirement of any profiled municipality.
municipalities.product_play: The product can automate generation of both the plan and the report — unusually high value for Breda.

scoring_system.description: 4-parameter scoring system. Sum scores → divide by 4 → assign participation level.

parameters.name: building_category
parameters.description: Number of housing units or commercial space volume

parameters.name: traffic_congestion_effects
parameters.description: Impact on traffic flow and congestion

parameters.name: green_space_impact
parameters.description: Impact on green spaces and ecology

parameters.name: neighbourhood_character_change
parameters.description: Changes to street profile and neighbourhood character

levels.label: Minimal impact
levels.description: Inform affected parties with basic project information

levels.label: Moderate impact
levels.description: Structured input and discussion with stakeholders

levels.label: Major impact
levels.description: Stakeholders actively involved in shaping the plan

municipalities.document_notes: Report must cover: steps taken, results, support levels, how feedback shaped the plan
municipalities.strictness_notes: Score-based like Amsterdam — replicable pattern. Level 1/2/3 based on 4-parameter impact score.
municipalities.product_play: Arnhem and Amsterdam both use scoring systems — this is a replicable architecture across multiple municipalities.
research_gaps: Exact scoring scale per parameter (e.g., how many points for 10 housing units vs 50?)

binary_list_system.description: Participation mandatory for exactly those activities where the council holds binding advisory rights. CVDR696321 states explicitly: 'Participatie is alleen verplicht voor die activiteiten waarbij de raad een bindend adviesrecht heeft.' One lookup, two flags: mandatory participation (Art. 16.55) AND council binding advisory right (Art. 16.15a) — same list.

municipalities.document_notes: Per the Participatiebeleid voor het ruimtelijk domein van de gemeente Nijmegen
municipalities.policy_document_type: Aanwijzingsbesluit verplichte participatie + Participatiebeleid ruimtelijk domein
municipalities.strictness_notes: Compact list — 6 categories. Housing trigger is low (≥2 units) meaning most residential developments qualify. Energy and infrastructure always mandatory.
municipalities.product_play: Nijmegen is now fully modellable. Model the list once → surface two product features simultaneously (participation flag + council advisory flag).

broad_trigger_system.description: Mandatory when a legal statute requires it OR when 'reasonably expectable stakeholders' exist. Broad trigger means almost any project with affected neighbours requires participation.

municipalities.parolo_data_note: Parolo incorrectly lists Haarlem as 'geen beleid' — Parolo is outdated. Haarlem has a 2024 ordinance.
municipalities.strictness_notes: Detailed documentation requirements among the most demanding of all profiled municipalities.
municipalities.product_play: Participatieverslag generator highly valuable here — Haarlem's required documentation is unusually detailed.

dialogue_system.description: When a plan doesn't fit the omgevingsplan (i.e., a BOPA is needed), the Bossche omgevingsdialoog is required. Flexible format — the initiator enters into dialogue with surrounding stakeholders.

municipalities.document_notes: No fixed template — document what was done and what came of it
municipalities.strictness_notes: Flexible format is harder to navigate than structured systems — no clear checklist of what 'sufficient' looks like.
municipalities.product_play: Product should clarify what 'sufficient dialogue' looks like in 's-Hertogenbosch context — filling the flexibility gap.
research_gaps: Minimum standards for 'sufficient' Bossche omgevingsdialoog — what does the municipality actually check for?

binary_list_system.description: 8 categories with measurable, programmatically evaluable thresholds

categories.name: vision_conflicts
categories.description: Projects contradicting the municipal environmental vision
categories.notes: Judgment call — flag for user to check with municipality

categories.name: housing_urban
categories.threshold: 5 or more homes in one project, urban location

categories.name: housing_rural
categories.threshold: 5 or more homes in rural areas

categories.name: eia_required
categories.description: Projects requiring mandatory Environmental Impact Assessment (m.e.r.)

categories.name: sensitive_nature_areas
categories.threshold: construction in 'waardevol' or 'zeer waardevol' zones on municipal nature map

municipalities.strictness_notes: Thresholds are precise and programmatically evaluable — simplest to build a checker for.
municipalities.product_play: Input project specs → check against thresholds → binary output. Best first binary-list implementation.

binary_list_system.description: 7 categories (with subcategories). On the list → mandatory. Off the list → voluntary. Adopted May 25, 2022. Effective January 1, 2024. List to be evaluated after 2 years.

subcategories.threshold: Any — within or outside bebouwde kom

subcategories.activity: Woning volume extension — outside bebouwde kom

subcategories.threshold: > 500m² BVO (exempt: business parks; <500m²; nieuwe economische dragers; home-based)

subcategories.activity: Holiday park area or unit count expansion
Expansion of bouwmassa of recreational units

subcategories.threshold: College judgment — NOT a hard requirement; always flag for manual review
subcategories.flag_note: Always produce a soft warning in checker output — municipality may require participation even if no other category triggered

municipalities.document_notes: Statement of whether and how stakeholders were involved, and what results emerged
municipalities.policy_document_type: Verordening lijst specifieke gevallen waarbij participatie voor een aanvraag BOPA verplicht is
municipalities.strictness_notes: 7-category list with detailed subcategories. Categories 1–6 are fully rule-based and programmatically checkable. Category 7 is a soft catch-all.
municipalities.product_play: Fully modellable. Model categories 1–6 programmatically; always surface category 7 as a soft warning.

checklist_system.description: No explicit mandatory trigger stated. Developer determines scope using the municipality's 6-step checklist. Proportional to project scale and environmental impact.

steps.title: Invite participants
contents: clarity on what is fixed vs. flexible in the plan

municipalities.document_notes: Report must include: methodology, attendance, changes made, satisfaction ratings
municipalities.strictness_notes: Scale-based proportionality — no hard threshold. Developer self-determines scope using checklist.
municipalities.product_play: Maastricht's 6-step checklist format is close to the ideal participatieverslag generator flow.

binary_list_system.description: Mandatory participation for all BOPAs where the municipal council has advisory rights. The council designated specific categories via a policy rule on advisory rights adopted July 7, 2023. Participation obligation applies to exactly those same categories — one lookup, two flags (same pattern as Nijmegen).
binary_list_system.source: CVDR711762 — Notitie Participatie en participatieregels onder de Omgevingswet
binary_list_system.categories_note: Categories exist in the July 7, 2023 advisory rights policy rule but have not yet been extracted. Priority: fetch CVDR711762 and map the full list.

municipalities.document_notes: Two documents required: participatieplan (before — who, when, how) + participatieverslag (after — who, what was said, how feedback shaped the plan). Same two-document pattern as Breda.
municipalities.policy_document_type: Notitie Participatie en participatieregels onder de Omgevingswet
municipalities.strictness_notes: Two-document requirement + binary list trigger. High compliance burden once categories are known.
municipalities.product_play: Apeldoorn's two-document requirement is the same pattern as Breda — high-value output. Fetch the July 7, 2023 advisory rights list to complete the checker.
research_gaps: Extract specific BOPA categories from the July 7, 2023 advisory rights policy rule (CVDR711762)

opt_out.description: Leiden's policy rules explicitly state: no participation obligation applies to environmental permit applications for BOPAs. Participation is entirely voluntary.

municipalities.document_notes: No mandatory documentation required. Voluntary participation may be documented at developer's discretion.
municipalities.strictness_notes: Only profiled municipality that has completely opted out of mandatory BOPA participation.
municipalities.product_play: Must surface this clearly. Developers from other cities assume mandatory = everywhere. Leiden is a significant exception that prevents unnecessary work.

binary_list_system.description: 6 categories trigger BOTH mandatory participation (Art. 16.55) AND council advisory rights (Art. 16.15a). 2 additional categories trigger advisory rights only (NOT mandatory participation). All apply only when project conflicts with the omgevingsplan (BOPA condition). Exception: if council previously approved a stedenbouwkundig plan, ontwikkelplan, or programma for the development, no advisory right required.
binary_list_system.source: gmb-2024-63914 — Lijst verzwaard advies en participatieplicht gemeente Delft

municipalities.document_notes: Per Delft's environmental dialogue guideline (adopted Oct 31, 2023 by college). Previous error: earlier profile was based on outdated 2022 pre-OW verordening (CVDR669329) — correct document is gmb-2024-63914.
municipalities.policy_document_type: Lijst verzwaard advies en participatieplicht gemeente Delft
municipalities.strictness_notes: 6 mandatory participation triggers, all programmatically checkable. The adviesrecht-only categories (7, 8) are an important nuance — product must flag both separately.
municipalities.product_play: Fully modellable. 6 hard triggers + 2 soft adviesrecht flags. Worth modelling Delft as a second binary-list showcase (alongside Groningen).

guidance_system.description: No formal aanwijzingsbesluit under Art. 16.55 lid 7 OW found. Participation obligation for larger projects arises from the municipal Participatiegids guidance, not a legal designation. Non-compliance does not automatically trigger rejection under OW, but may block permit progress in practice.

tiers.examples: new housing developments, solar fields, significant construction

guidance_system.previous_error: Earlier profile was based on the 2009 inspraakverordening (CVDR8423) which predates the Omgevingswet entirely

municipalities.document_notes: Participatiedocument based on the Participatiegids fixed steps. Required for larger projects.
municipalities.strictness_notes: Guidance-based, no formal legal designation. Larger projects have fixed process steps that are de facto mandatory. Smaller projects: inform and document.
municipalities.product_play: Amersfoort is a process guidance play — walk developers through the participatiegids steps and generate the participatiedocument.
research_gaps: Exact Participatiegids process steps and thresholds for 'larger' vs 'smaller' projects — need to read the July 2024 PDF

binary_list_system.description: One of the broadest designation lists found. 9 categories. All apply when projects 'do not fit within a spatial-planning framework' established by the municipal council (BOPA condition). Key characteristic: any single additional housing unit triggers mandatory participation.
binary_list_system.source: CVDR705837 — Lijst van gevallen verplichte participatie buitenplanse omgevingsplanactiviteiten

categories.name: woningbouw
categories.threshold: Toevoeging van één of meerdere woningen (any housing unit addition)
categories.note: Widest housing trigger of all profiled municipalities — single unit triggers it

categories.name: energie
categories.threshold: Wind turbines (any), solar fields (any scale), alternative energy storage

categories.name: antennes
categories.threshold: Installing or modifying antenna installations (any height)

municipalities.document_notes: Participation documentation per municipality's six guiding principles: early engagement, transparency, genuine dialogue.
municipalities.policy_document_type: Lijst van gevallen verplichte participatie buitenplanse omgevingsplanactiviteiten
municipalities.strictness_notes: Broadest list found in the Netherlands. 'Any housing unit' trigger means almost every residential developer in Venlo must participate. Energy and antennas: any scale.
municipalities.product_play: High-value municipality. Breadth of list means almost every developer needs guidance. The single-unit housing trigger will surprise many developers who assume participation is only for large projects.
research_gaps: Exact thresholds for 'large-scale' community services (cat. 2), sports amenities (cat. 3), and intensive recreation (cat. 4) — need to read CVDR705837 in full

binary_list_system.description: Mandatory participation for projects involving ≥26 dwellings that conflict with the environmental plan (BOPA condition). Additional categories may be designated by the council in the full Participatieverordening 2022.
binary_list_system.categories_note: ≥26 dwellings is the confirmed mandatory trigger. Additional categories likely exist in the full Participatieverordening but have not been extracted.
binary_list_system.guide: In gesprek met je omgeving — available on doemee.zoetermeer.nl. Covers: who to consult, when, methods, how to use results.

municipalities.document_notes: Must document: manner of participation, participants involved, feedback received, how feedback influenced modifications, specific plan adjustments made. Missing → municipality will not process the application.
municipalities.strictness_notes: ≥26 dwelling trigger is clean and programmatically evaluable. Civic engagement culture (doemee platform) means developers who ignore it get pushback.
municipalities.product_play: Clean, product-friendly rule. One threshold → binary output. Zoetermeer is an easy win to model.
research_gaps: Full Participatieverordening 2022 — extract any additional BOPA categories beyond the ≥26 dwellings trigger

guidance_system.description: No formal aanwijzingsbesluit designating specific BOPA categories under Art. 16.55 lid 7 OW found publicly. Participation is strongly expected and recommended for all spatial initiatives, but non-compliance is not an automatic rejection trigger (unless a not-yet-found aanwijzingsbesluit exists).
guidance_system.slogan: Over de kracht van de samenleving
guidance_system.disclosure_requirement: At permit submission: indicate whether/how/what participation occurred (Art. 16.55 lid 1 — applies everywhere)
guidance_system.handboek_note: A 'Handboek BOPA en wijzigen omgevingsplan' is available by email request (gemeente@helmond.nl) — not published publicly. May contain a formal BOPA participation designation.

municipalities.document_notes: Standard disclosure at submission: whether, how, and what happened with participation outcomes.
municipalities.strictness_notes: Guidance-based — similar to Zaanstad and Amersfoort. Participation expected and recommended; non-compliance not automatically fatal.
municipalities.product_play: Helmond is a guidance play. The withheld Handboek is a product opportunity — developers shouldn't need to email the municipality to find out the rules.
research_gaps: Request Handboek BOPA via gemeente@helmond.nl — may contain a formal BOPA designation list
Check if a separate aanwijzingsbesluit has been published

binary_list_system.description: Designation list exists — confirmed by CVDR724190 (UOV beleidsregel) which explicitly references 'de lijst van 22 december 2021 waarvoor zowel een bindend advies van de gemeenteraad conform artikel 16.15a sub b onder 1° van de Omgevingswet én participatie zoals bedoeld in artikel 16.55 Ow is vereist.' Council adopted the list December 22, 2021. Effective January 1, 2024. Same combined-list pattern as Nijmegen.
sources: CVDR724190 (Beleidsregel toepassing uitgebreide procedure BOPA — references the list)
binary_list_system.categories_note: Specific BOPA categories from the December 22, 2021 raadsbesluit have NOT yet been extracted. Priority: find and read the Dec 22, 2021 raadsbesluit to extract the category list.

broad_trigger.description: Art. 2.1 of the verordening sets a broad trigger: participation applies whenever 'reasonably expectable stakeholders exist who are significantly affected.' This applies even to projects NOT on the designation list.
broad_trigger.two_level_guidance: Level 1: check designation list → mandatory if on it. Level 2: check if 'reasonably expectable stakeholders' exist → mandatory even if not on the list.

municipalities.document_notes: Eindverslag per Art. 5 of the verordening: overview of process, summary of stakeholder input, list of agreements made, response to input received.
municipalities.policy_document_type: Verordening participatie Omgevingswet gemeente Leeuwarden 2024 + Beleidsregel toepassing uitgebreide procedure BOPA
municipalities.strictness_notes: Broad trigger (Art. 2.1) means even projects not on the designation list may need participation. Two-level compliance check required.
municipalities.product_play: Leeuwarden requires two-level guidance: (a) on the list → definitely mandatory, (b) off the list → check if stakeholders exist. High complexity for developers.
research_gaps: Extract the specific BOPA categories from the December 22, 2021 raadsbesluit
Confirm CVDR number for the designation list itself

guidance_system.description: No formal aanwijzingsbesluit designating specific BOPA categories under Art. 16.55 lid 7 OW. The protocol enforces the general Art. 16.55 lid 1 OW obligation (applies to ALL municipalities): disclose whether/how/what consultation occurred. Zaanstad's protocol is particularly emphatic: 'Als er sprake is van een afwijking van het omgevingsplan, dan is participatie echt cruciaal.'
guidance_system.source: Zaans Participatieprotocol — versie april 2024, geactualiseerd na invoering Omgevingswet
guidance_system.previous_error: Earlier research captured the wrong document (Re-integratieverordening Participatiewet — social welfare law). Correct document: Zaans Participatieprotocol.

four_step_process.title: Delen, uitwerken en toetsen
four_step_process.description: Document ideas, concerns, solutions; test back with the group

four_step_process.title: Pas plannen aan
four_step_process.description: Adjust the plan; complete the participatiedocument for the permit application

guidance_system.disclosure_requirement: At permit submission: whether (of), how (hoe), what was done with outcomes (wat). Standard Art. 16.55 lid 1 obligation.

municipalities.document_notes: Participatiedocument — the filled-in result of Stap 4 of the 4-step process.
municipalities.strictness_notes: Strong expectation of participation; no formal legal designation. Participation quality may influence permit outcome but non-participation does not automatically trigger rejection.
municipalities.product_play: Zaanstad developers need a guided tool for the 4-step process and a participatiedocument generator. Pure process guidance value.

pending_system.description: Participatieverordening Deventer 2026 (Art. 16.3) explicitly commits the council to establishing a BOPA participation designation list — but that list does NOT yet exist as a separate published document. Developers are in legal limbo.
pending_system.what_exists: General participation framework: inwonersparticipatie, inspraak, overheidsparticipatie, uitdaagrecht. Detailed participatieplan and participatieverslag requirements. Hardheidsclausule allows deviation in special cases.
pending_system.what_is_missing: The BOPA aanwijzingsbesluit promised in Art. 16.3 — not yet published as of April 20, 2026.

municipalities.document_notes: No formal BOPA participation documentation requirement identified yet. Apply national safe minimum until the designation list is published.
municipalities.strictness_notes: Framework committed, BOPA list not yet published. Ambiguity = developer uncertainty = product value once the list appears.
municipalities.product_play: Flag Deventer as 'BOPA participation requirement unclear — check with municipality.' Monitor for the aanwijzingsbesluit.
research_gaps: Monitor lokaleregelgeving.overheid.nl for Deventer's BOPA aanwijzingsbesluit under Art. 16.3 of Participatieverordening 2026

guidance_system.description: The verordening explicitly excludes omgevingsvergunning (BOPA) applications from its scope. Direct quote: 'De bepalingen uit hoofdstuk 4 van de verordening over overheidsparticipatie zijn niet van toepassing bij initiatieven in de fysieke leefomgeving van gemeente Ede waar een omgevingsvergunning voor nodig is.' Developers directed to separate 'Handreiking Betrek de omgeving' for voluntary guidance.
guidance_system.handreiking_note: Handreiking 'Betrek de omgeving' is the voluntary step-by-step guide. Not legally binding. Content not yet extracted.

municipalities.document_notes: No mandatory documentation required under the verordening. Voluntary participation may be documented per the handreiking.
municipalities.strictness_notes: Explicit exclusion of BOPA applications from the verordening. Probably the lowest-obligation mid-size municipality found after Leiden.
municipalities.product_play: Ede is a 'safe' municipality for complex projects — no mandatory participation trigger. Surface this explicitly to developers who assume mandatory = everywhere.
research_gaps: Find and read the Handreiking 'Betrek de omgeving' to understand what Ede's voluntary guidance actually recommends

discretionary_system.description: General participation framework with Omgevingswet applicability. No specific BOPA designation list. Participation level determined case-by-case using the 'Dordtse Participatie Praatplaat' (afwegingskader).
discretionary_system.ow_applicability: Art. 2.2: 'Het bestuursorgaan past bij toepassing van de Omgevingswet, zoveel mogelijk deze verordening toe'
discretionary_system.afwegingskader: Dordtse Participatie Praatplaat — a decision framework guiding which participation level is appropriate for a given project

municipalities.document_notes: Documentation expected per general Art. 16.55 lid 1 OW obligation. Format and depth determined by Praatplaat level chosen.
municipalities.strictness_notes: No binary trigger list. Participation level is case-by-case judgment using the Praatplaat. Medium complexity for developers.
municipalities.product_play: Model the Praatplaat decision logic to give developers meaningful guidance. Check for a separate BOPA aanwijzingsbesluit under Art. 16.55 OW.
research_gaps: Find whether Dordrecht has published a separate BOPA aanwijzingsbesluit under Art. 16.55 OW
Map the Dordtse Participatie Praatplaat decision logic

binary_list_system.description: Mandatory participation for housing developments of ≥3 units that conflict with the omgevingsplan. Standard BOPA condition applies.

municipalities.document_notes: 4-part required format: stakeholders, aanpak, resultaten, verwerking.
municipalities.strictness_notes: Standard binary-list approach with clear housing threshold. No scoring system.
research_gaps: Extract full category list beyond housing trigger

discretionary_system.description: Strongly agricultural municipality (greenhouse region). No formal BOPA designation list published. Participation level determined via mandatory pre-consultation (vooroverleg) with municipal team. Agri-specific context means many projects are evaluated individually.

municipalities.document_notes: As determined per project in vooroverleg.
municipalities.strictness_notes: Case-by-case approach. Agricultural area means many projects have unique contexts — vooroverleg is essential.
research_gaps: Confirm whether a formal BOPA aanwijzingsbesluit exists
Exact participation requirements for standard residential vs agricultural projects

guidance_system.description: No formal BOPA designation list. Participation strongly encouraged but not designated as mandatory under Art. 16.55 lid 7 OW. Developer must still comply with the national Art. 16.55 lid 1 obligation: disclose at submission whether, how, and with what result participation occurred.

municipalities.document_notes: Disclosure at submission: whether (of), how (hoe), and what was done with outcomes (wat).
municipalities.strictness_notes: Voluntary framework. Disclosure obligation is national baseline, not Emmen-specific.
research_gaps: Confirm no BOPA aanwijzingsbesluit exists under Art. 16.55 lid 7
Any upcoming policy change

binary_list_system.description: Mandatory participation for projects conflicting with the omgevingsplan that meet size or housing count thresholds. Dense peri-urban municipality with significant housing development pressure.

municipalities.document_notes: Must cover: stakeholder identification, approach used, feedback received, and how feedback shaped the plan.
municipalities.policy_document_type: Aanwijzingsbesluit verplichte participatie BOPA — Gemeente Haarlemmermeer
municipalities.strictness_notes: Housing pressure municipality. Low threshold (5 units) means most residential BOPAs trigger mandatory participation.
research_gaps: Confirm full category list from CVDR706812
Verify exact BVO threshold for non-residential

blanket_system.description: All applications conflicting with the omgevingsplan (BOPAs) require participation. Municipality provides a step-by-step guideline to structure the process.

municipalities.document_notes: Process verslag: stepwise account of who was involved, what was discussed, and how input influenced the plan.
municipalities.strictness_notes: Blanket trigger — every BOPA requires participation. No threshold exceptions found.
research_gaps: Verify whether a scale-based intensity system exists within the blanket requirement

binary_list_system.description: Mandatory participation for housing developments of ≥4 units or other construction over 500m² BVO that conflict with the omgevingsplan.

municipalities.document_notes: Participatieverslag covering approach, stakeholders, feedback received, and how it was processed.
municipalities.policy_document_type: Aanwijzingsbesluit verplichte participatie — Gemeente Alphen aan den Rijn
municipalities.strictness_notes: Clear binary thresholds. Low housing trigger (4 units) captures most residential developments.
research_gaps: Confirm full category list from CVDR704938
Any additional triggers beyond housing/scale

guidance_system.description: No formal BOPA designation list found. Participation recommended and encouraged but not designated mandatory under Art. 16.55 lid 7 OW. Developer must still provide the national Art. 16.55 lid 1 disclosure at submission.

municipalities.document_notes: Standard disclosure: whether, how, and with what result participation occurred.
municipalities.strictness_notes: Voluntary framework. Similar to Emmen — national baseline disclosure only.
research_gaps: Confirm no BOPA aanwijzingsbesluit exists
Check for any upcoming policy changes

discretionary_system.description: No formal BOPA designation list published. Participation level determined per project in mandatory pre-consultation with the municipal project team. Historic city centre context means many projects are sensitive regardless of scale.

municipalities.document_notes: Format and scope determined per project in vooroverleg.
municipalities.strictness_notes: Case-by-case in historic context. Sensitive urban fabric means participation expectations run high even without formal designation.
research_gaps: Confirm no formal BOPA aanwijzingsbesluit
Map the vooroverleg process and what triggers stronger participation requirements

binary_list_system.description: Mandatory participation for housing developments of ≥3 units or other construction over 500m² BVO that conflict with the omgevingsplan.

municipalities.document_notes: Must describe approach, involved parties, feedback received, and how feedback shaped the plan.
municipalities.policy_document_type: Aanwijzingsbesluit verplichte participatie BOPA — Gemeente Roosendaal
municipalities.strictness_notes: Low housing threshold (3 units) means most residential developments trigger mandatory participation.
research_gaps: Confirm full category list from CVDR700182
Verify whether commercial/institutional thresholds exist beyond the 500m² rule

discretionary_system.description: No formal BOPA designation list found. Participation requirements determined case-by-case through mandatory pre-consultation. Planned city with ongoing large-scale development means many projects involve complex participation expectations.

municipalities.document_notes: Format and depth determined per project in vooroverleg.
municipalities.strictness_notes: Case-by-case. Large ongoing urban development projects often have de facto participation expectations regardless of formal obligation.
research_gaps: Confirm no formal BOPA aanwijzingsbesluit
Review participation protocol for large-scale housing projects

binary_list_system.description: Mandatory participation for housing developments of ≥4 units or other construction over 600m² BVO conflicting with the omgevingsplan.

municipalities.document_notes: Covers who was involved, the approach used, results, and how feedback influenced plan modifications.
municipalities.policy_document_type: Aanwijzingsbesluit verplichte participatie BOPA — Gemeente Purmerend
municipalities.strictness_notes: Standard binary-list. Growing municipality with active housing programme.
research_gaps: Confirm full category list from CVDR702847

guidance_system.description: No formal BOPA designation list found. Participation encouraged, with Art. 16.55 lid 1 national disclosure obligation applying at submission. Historic harbour city — participation culture high even where not legally required.

municipalities.document_notes: Standard disclosure at submission: whether, how, and with what results.
municipalities.strictness_notes: Voluntary framework. Disclosure at submission is national baseline.
research_gaps: Confirm no formal BOPA aanwijzingsbesluit
Check for upcoming policy changes given housing pressure in the region

guidance_system.description: No formal BOPA designation list found. Participation encouraged under the municipality's general participation framework. Developer must provide the Art. 16.55 lid 1 national disclosure at permit submission.

municipalities.document_notes: Standard disclosure at submission: whether, how, and with what result.
municipalities.strictness_notes: Voluntary framework. National baseline disclosure obligation applies.
research_gaps: Confirm no formal BOPA aanwijzingsbesluit under Art. 16.55 lid 7
Review any recent policy updates

national_safe_minimum.description: When no municipal policy exists, apply this national safe minimum based on Art. 16.55 Omgevingswet
national_safe_minimum.warning: No published policy — use safe minimum and consider proactively contacting the municipality to ask what they expect
